title: Verifier Gated by Coder Evidence Ceiling
claim: The verifier assignment is valid and the required independent-verification control is reachable at this risk level.

observed_evidence.detail: A coder-derived execution-evidence ceiling was applied to verifier candidate eligibility; verifier routes carry no coder execution-evidence receipts, so their effective ceiling collapsed to low.
At risk >= medium every otherwise-capable verifier route was excluded with risk_above_supported_ceiling, leaving the verifier-eligible universe empty; the coder-role output was unaffected.

missing_evidence.detail: No proof that the required independent-verification control is reachable; the absence of an eligible verifier is being treated as though no capable verifier exists.
No evidence that verifier eligibility was computed from canonical verifier registry review-capability facts rather than from coder execution evidence.

required_action: Make the ceiling computation role-aware: retain min(registry, evidence) for the coder role and keep coder output byte-for-byte identical; for the verifier role use the canonical registry review-capability ceiling, treat canonical not-applicable/unrestricted values per existing registry semantics, and fail closed on malformed verifier capability data without requiring coder runtime-evidence receipts. Prove the eligible verifier universe is non-empty where canonical capability permits. Block the assignment or use corrected role-specific capability semantics; do not read an empty verifier universe as proof that no capable verifier exists.
notes: REPRESENTATIVE_EXAMPLE — NOT_A_VERIFIED_HISTORICAL_INCIDENT. The narrowest justified class (VERIFIER_GATED_BY_CODER_EVIDENCE_CEILING); the broader ROLE_SEMANTIC_RISK_CEILING_CONFLATION phrasing is not adopted, as no second distinct instance is observed. Distilled from internal CAP Processor forensic evidence; no commit id, path, digest, receipt, timestamp, or production landing is asserted as canonical fact. CODER_EVIDENCE_CEILING does not imply VERIFIER_REVIEW_CAPABILITY, and SCORER_RETURNED_ROWS does not imply REQUIRED_VERIFIER_CONTROL_REACHABLE. This is NOT a gateway authority-context failure (AICE-602), NOT a bypassable/unbound control (AICE-610), NOT component-vs-end-to-end reachability substitution (AICE-611), and NOT an infrastructure failure recorded as a semantic verdict (AICE-614): infrastructure retries may occur during repair but are not this class. The incident requires the coder path to remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
